title: Orbits, Bentos, Gradients, Tilt & Counters
description: CSS offset-path planets, :has() responsive bento grid, @property gradient text, 3D tilt cards, and scroll-triggered counters.

drops.title: CSS Offset-Path Orbits
drops.description: Planets glide along elliptical SVG paths using CSS offset-path — silky 60fps motion with zero JavaScript.
drops.concept: The CSS offset-path property lets any element follow a defined SVG path using offset-distance (0%→100%) animated with @keyframes. Combined with offset-rotate:auto the element naturally faces its direction of travel. This creates physics-feeling motion paths impossible with transform: translate alone.
drops.source: Modern CSS Daily · MDN offset-path docs

drops.title: CSS :has() Smart Bento
drops.description: A bento grid that reshapes its column widths on hover using CSS :has() selectors — no JavaScript whatsoever.
drops.concept: The :has() pseudo-class enables parent selectors in CSS for the first time, letting a grid container change its layout when a child is in a specific state. Hovering any card triggers :has(.card:nth-child(N):hover) on the grid, which adjusts grid-template-columns to expand that column. All dimming and scaling of siblings is pure CSS.
drops.source: Riad Kilani — CSS Features 2025 · web.dev :has() docs

interaction.mouse: Hover any card to expand its column and dim siblings

drops.title: @property Gradient Text Animation
drops.description: CSS registered custom properties animate gradient stop positions — creating flows and pulses impossible with standard CSS.
drops.concept: @property lets you register a typed custom property with an initial value, unlocking animation of things like <percentage> and <angle> values. Applied to gradient stop positions and combined with background-clip:text, this creates gradient text that flows, reverses, and pulses smoothly at 60fps with zero JS. Four style variants are shown.
drops.source: web.dev — Speedy CSS Tip: Animated Gradient Text

interaction.click: Speed slider and pause toggle in controls

drops.title: 3D Mouse-Tilt Cards
drops.description: Cards tilt toward your cursor in real-time using perspective + rotateX/Y, with a specular sheen that tracks mouse position.
drops.concept: Mouse position is sampled on mousemove, normalized to 0..1 across the card, and mapped to rotateX and rotateY angles. CSS perspective(900px) wraps the card, and transform-style:preserve-3d lets the content layer float at translateZ(20px) for a parallax depth effect. A radial-gradient sheen overlay repositions its center to match the mouse, simulating a moving light source.
drops.source: letsbuildui.dev — 3D Hover Effect Using CSS Transforms

interaction.mouse: Move cursor over any card to tilt it toward you

drops.title: Scroll-Triggered Counters
drops.description: Numbers count up with ease-out cubic when scrolled into view — IntersectionObserver fires once per element, no libraries.
drops.concept: IntersectionObserver (threshold:0.35) fires a callback when a counter enters the viewport. A requestAnimationFrame loop then increments from 0 to the target value over a configurable duration, applying an ease-out cubic easing function. Large numbers auto-format to M/B abbreviations. Paired with a CSS progress bar that transitions width on entry for a secondary visual indicator.
drops.source: getbutterfly.com — Animated JavaScript Counter-Up with IntersectionObserver API
